reasoning.typography: Generic sans-serif (Camera Plain Variable) lacks character — could be any SaaS. No typographic tension or personality, just safe legibility.
reasoning.hierarchy: Headline dominates adequately, but the gradient background competes for attention. The input field is properly subordinate, though the 'New' badge disrupts focus.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Pure AI-tool stencil: swap 'Lovable' for 'BuildFast' or 'CodeGen' and nothing changes. The gradient + input pattern is 2023-24 boilerplate.
reasoning.color: Vibrant gradient is decorative eye-candy, not disciplined. Occupies ~70% of viewport — the opposite of restrained accent use. No clear color system beyond 'make it pop'.
reasoning.density: Massive void above the headline wastes space. Uniform padding on nav. The hero takes standard 'center everything' approach without confident density decisions.
reasoning.voice: Structure is pure SaaS template: hero with input + CTA. 'Build something Lovable' is brand-first, not story-first. IA invisible — where's the why/how/proof?

winning_patterns: Clear visual dominance of the main headline — no confusion about where to look first
The gradient provides energy and consumer-facing warmth vs. enterprise sterility
Dark nav bar creates clean separation from hero content
anti_patterns: AI-tool template #47: gradient background + centered input field + vague AI promise
Typographic system has zero personality — pure commodity sans-serif
Massive undifferentiated whitespace above headline wastes prime viewport real estate
Color gradient is decorative wallpaper, not a disciplined accent strategy
IA completely hidden — no story structure visible on hero screen
